steps.title: Use the view in context
steps.content: Moss points toward the windows as the Port of Oakland passes by, and the narrator says he has never tired of this specific view. In context, specific means this particular view, not a general or imagined one.

steps.title: Reject mismatched meanings
steps.content: Choice A is wrong because the view is real, not imaginary. Choice B is wrong because energetic describes activity, not which view is meant. Choice C is wrong because correct does not fit the sentence's meaning. The answer is D.
